steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers found that bulkheads are more strongly negatively correlated with waterbird community integrity than riprap. To illustrate this finding, you need a choice that directly compares a high-bulkhead site to a high-riprap site and shows the bulkhead site scoring lower on the IWCI.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Waterbird communities at Curtis, a site with a high percentage of shoreline consisting of bulkheads and riprap, had lower average IWCI scores than did waterbird communities at Onancock, a site with a low percentage of shoreline consisting of bulkheads and riprap.: Compares a mixed-hardening site to a low-hardening site. It never isolates bulkheads versus riprap, so it cannot show that bulkheads are worse than riprap. ✗
  • Choice B — The difference in average IWCI scores for waterbird communities at Stony and Old Road, two sites with a higher percentage of shoreline consisting of bulkheads than of riprap, was statistically insignificant.: Both sites have more bulkheads than riprap, so there is no bulkhead-versus-riprap contrast at all. A comparison within the same type proves nothing about which type is more harmful. ✗
  • Choice C — Waterbird communities at Old Road, a site with a relatively high percentage of shoreline consisting of bulkheads, had lower average IWCI scores than did waterbird communities at Miles, a site with a relatively high percentage of shoreline consisting of riprap.: Old Road is a high-bulkhead site and Miles is a high-riprap site. Old Road scoring lower directly shows that the bulkhead-heavy site has worse waterbird community integrity than the riprap-heavy site, exactly illustrating the researchers’ finding. ✓
  • Choice D — Waterbird communities at Curtis, a site with equal percentages of shoreline consisting of bulkheads and riprap, had higher average IWCI scores than did waterbird communities at Miles, a site with different percentages of shoreline consisting of bulkheads and riprap.: Comparing equal-percentage hardening to unequal-percentage hardening does not test bulkhead versus riprap. The outcome could be explained by total hardening amount, not type. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
